summary: Phase 3 audit of jfrog-devops at v1.1.1. Found 6 substantive content updates needed: (1) JFrog Pipelines EOL date is now in the past (May 1 2026) and copy needed past-tense; (2) Hugging Face legacy repo deprecation in June 2026 is a major new Israeli-relevance gotcha for ML teams; (3) JFrog ML and AI Catalog (from the Qwak acquisition in June 2024, not RunAI as the prompt suggested) are new platform pillars; (4) Xray SBOM/CBOM/VEX support has matured and deserves first-class coverage; (5) OIDC is now the JFrog-recommended GitHub Actions auth path; (6) JFrog CLI is at 2.103.0 (April 29 2026). Pricing tier ranges also added. All facts cross-verified against jfrog.com, github.com/jfrog, huggingface.co/blog, and investors.jfrog.com.

claims.claim: Latest JFrog CLI version is v2.103.0, released April 29, 2026
claims.evidence: GitHub releases page lists v2.103.0 (Apr 29 2026), v2.102.0 (Apr 21 2026), v2.101.0 (Apr 18 2026)

claims.claim: JFrog acquired Qwak in June 2024 for approximately $230M to add MLOps to its platform
claims.evidence: Press release + Ctech reporting confirm $230M Qwak acquisition. (RunAI was acquired by NVIDIA, not JFrog, contrary to the prompt's hypothesis.)

claims.claim: JFrog ML launched March 2025 as the first product from the Qwak acquisition
claims.evidence: BusinessWire/JFrog press release dated March 4 2025 announces JFrog ML

claims.claim: JFrog AI Catalog launched September 2025 for governing OpenAI, Anthropic, NVIDIA NIM, and Hugging Face access
claims.evidence: Sept 9 2025 BusinessWire / JFrog press release explicitly names OpenAI, Anthropic, NVIDIA NIM, Hugging Face integrations + Nemotron access

claims.claim: JFrog Pipelines reached end of life on May 1, 2026
claims.evidence: Multiple JFrog help-center pages and third-party trackers (invgate.com itdb) confirm May 1 2026 EOL. Today is 2026-05-20, so EOL is in the past.

claims.claim: Legacy Hugging Face repository type in Artifactory deprecates June 2026; migration to new Machine Learning layout required
claims.evidence: Hugging Face engineering blog (jeffboudier) details June 2026 migration deadline, one-way nature, restore_layout caveat, and federation constraints

claims.claim: New Machine Learning repository type introduced in Artifactory 7.111.1+ supports Hugging Face + PyTorch + ONNX + .pkl + .joblib + .pth + .cbm with FrogML SDK and Xet protocol
claims.evidence: Both Hugging Face blog and JFrog docs list the file types, version, FrogML SDK, and Xet support

claims.claim: Xray exports SBOMs in SPDX and CycloneDX formats; CycloneDX includes VEX data from Xray 3.67+
claims.evidence: JFrog security docs explicitly cover SPDX (ISO/IEC standard) and CycloneDX exports, plus VEX integration from Xray 3.67.x

claims.claim: Xray 3.131+ embeds CBOM (Cryptography Bill of Materials) into CycloneDX exports when JFrog Advanced Security secrets scanning is enabled
claims.evidence: Release notes confirm 3.131 CBOM addition; documentation describes the CycloneDX + secrets payload

claims.claim: OIDC is the JFrog-recommended GitHub Actions auth method; requires JFrog CLI 2.75.0+ and id-token: write permission
claims.evidence: setup-jfrog-cli README and JFrog OIDC docs both call out CLI 2.75.0+ requirement and id-token: write permission

claims.claim: Legacy X-JFrog-Art-Api API keys reached end of life Q4 2024; disabled by default in Artifactory 7.98+
claims.evidence: Carried over from v1.1.1 of this skill; still consistent with current JFrog deprecation pages

claims.claim: Frogbot is a free Git scanner that runs SCA + SAST + IaC on PRs; latest releases through Dec 2025
claims.evidence: GitHub repo and JFrog docs describe SCA+SAST+IaC, PR commenting, fix-PR opening, and free-tier compatibility

claims.claim: JFrog Curation added Compliant Version Selection (CVS), waiver requests, MCP Servers public label, and VS Code integration in 2025-2026
claims.evidence: Security self-managed releases page details CVS expansion (Conda/NuGet/RubyGems/Pub/Composer/Debian/Ubuntu), waiver requests, MCP Servers label, VS Code remote-repo curation

claims.claim: JFrog SaaS Pro tier published at $150/month, Enterprise X at $950/month, Enterprise+ on quote; self-managed Pro X ~$27k/yr, Enterprise X ~$48k/yr
claims.evidence: Pricing page + third-party trackers cite these tier prices for 2026; quotes vary by consumption. Marked medium because pricing changes more often than docs.

claims.claim: JFrog SaaS regions remain a fixed list (us-east, us-west, eu-frankfurt, eu-west, ap-southeast); no Israel region
claims.evidence: Carried from v1.1.1; JFrog SaaS regions page lists fixed AWS regions, no Tel Aviv. AWS Tel Aviv (il-central-1) does exist for BYOL.

claims.claim: JFrog integrated its platform with NVIDIA Enterprise AI Factory for agentic AI workflows
claims.evidence: JFrog press release confirms NVIDIA Enterprise AI Factory partnership

claims.claim: Conan 2 (V2) package support is available in Artifactory
claims.evidence: JFrog docs page dedicated to Conan V2 support; CLI 2.101.0 release notes call out Conan additions

decisions_made: Did NOT change skill name or core scope; remains DevOps + artifact + security focused with new MLOps section.
Kept SKILL_HE.md technical body in technical Hebrew per skills-il convention (the 'non-developer Hebrew' rule scopes to descriptions, FAQ, etc., per MEMORY).
Bumped version 1.1.1 -> 1.2.0 (minor: substantial new ML/AI section + SBOM + OIDC section, no breaking changes).
Added 3 tags (mlops, sbom, oidc) in both HE and EN.
Did NOT remove the 'API keys EOL Q4 2024' gotcha or the Israel-specific gotchas; they are still accurate.
Did NOT rewrite the Python client classes (artifactory_client.py / xray_client.py); their REST endpoints are still current. No scripts/ changes this pass.
Corrected the prompt's RunAI vs Qwak confusion in audit notes (NVIDIA acquired RunAI; JFrog acquired Qwak).
out_of_scope: No update to scripts/artifactory_client.py or scripts/xray_client.py (no API endpoint changes detected).
No update to references/api-reference.md (endpoints still valid; could add SBOM endpoints in a follow-up if needed).
